name: flyway
description: Flyway is a database migration tool that supports versioned and repeatable migrations using SQL or Java, with strong emphasis on simplicity and convention over configuration

subcommands.name: migrate
subcommands.description: Apply all pending versioned migrations to the database in order. Repeatable migrations are applied after versioned migrations when their checksum changes.

options.name: -url
options.description: JDBC URL of the database to migrate

options.name: -schemas
options.description: Comma-separated list of schemas to manage

options.name: -table
options.description: Name of the schema history table (default: flyway_schema_history)

options.name: -locations
options.description: Comma-separated list of locations to scan for migrations

options.name: -target
options.description: Target version up to which migrations should be applied

options.name: -connectRetries
options.description: Number of retries for database connections

options.name: -mixed
options.description: Allow mixing transactional and non-transactional statements in a migration

options.name: -group
options.description: Group all pending migrations in a single transaction

options.name: -validateOnMigrate
options.description: Validate applied migrations against resolved ones

options.name: -baselineOnMigrate
options.description: Automatically baseline when migrating against a non-empty schema

options.name: -cherryPick
options.description: Apply only specific migrations by version

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Drop all objects (tables, views, procedures, triggers, etc.) in the configured schemas. WARNING: This is a destructive operation and should never be used against production databases.

options.name: -cleanDisabled
options.description: Set to false to enable clean (disabled by default for safety)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Display the details and status of all migrations, showing applied, pending, and failed migrations with their versions and descriptions.

options.name: -infoSinceDate
options.description: Show only migrations applied after this date

options.name: -infoSinceVersion
options.description: Show only migrations from this version onwards

subcommands.name: validate
subcommands.description: Validate applied migrations against the locally available ones. Detects accidental changes to already applied migrations and verifies checksums match.

options.name: -ignoreMigrationPatterns
options.description: Patterns for migrations to ignore during validation

subcommands.name: undo
subcommands.description: Undo the most recently applied versioned migration. Requires Flyway Teams/Enterprise edition. Executes the undo script associated with the migration.

options.name: -target
options.description: Target version to undo down to

subcommands.name: baseline
subcommands.description: Baseline an existing database, marking it as the starting point for future Flyway migrations. Introduces Flyway to a pre-existing database.

options.name: -baselineVersion
options.description: Version to tag the existing schema with (default: 1)

options.name: -baselineDescription
options.description: Description to tag the existing schema with

subcommands.name: repair
subcommands.description: Repair the schema history table by removing failed migration entries (for databases without DDL transactions) and realigning checksums and descriptions.

subcommands.name: snapshot
subcommands.description: Capture a snapshot of the database schema structure for later comparison or diffing. Requires Flyway Teams edition.

options.name: -url
options.description: JDBC URL of the database to snapshot

options.name: -schemas
options.description: Schemas to include in the snapshot

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Compare two database schemas, migration directories, or snapshots and show the differences. Requires Flyway Teams edition.

options.name: -diff.source
options.description: Source for comparison (url, empty, migrations, snapshot)

options.name: -diff.artifactFilename
options.description: Output file for the generated diff migration

options.name: -diff.buildUrl
options.description: Database URL for the target environment

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Check for schema drift, pending changes, or perform dry-run analysis. Supports sub-operations: changes, drift, and dry-run. Requires Flyway Teams edition.

options.name: -drift
options.description: Check for drift between the database and applied migrations

options.name: -dryrun
options.description: Perform a dry-run of pending migrations without applying them

options.name: -check.buildUrl
options.description: URL for a clean build database for comparison

options.name: -check.reportFilename
options.description: Path for the check report file

globalOptions.name: -driver
globalOptions.description: Fully qualified class name of the JDBC driver

globalOptions.name: -schemas
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of schemas managed by Flyway

globalOptions.name: -table
globalOptions.description: Name of the schema history table

globalOptions.name: -locations
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of locations to scan for migrations

globalOptions.name: -configFiles
globalOptions.description: Comma-separated list of configuration files to load

globalOptions.name: -encoding
globalOptions.description: Encoding of SQL migration files (default: UTF-8)

globalOptions.name: -placeholders
globalOptions.description: Placeholders to replace in SQL migrations (key=value format)

globalOptions.name: -environment
globalOptions.description: The environment to use from TOML config
